stories.title: Megan Grant ESPYs — The Night Is Here
stories.angle: The show is tonight. The votes closed today. All the pushing, sharing, and voting led to this moment.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
THE ESPYS ARE TONIGHT.

Megan Grant is nominated for Best Record-Breaking Performance.

42 HR. A 31-year NCAA record. Beaten at the plate this year.

The show airs at 8 PM ET on ABC. Tonight is the night.

#ESPYs #Softball #MeganGrant

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
The 34th ESPYs are live right now on ABC.

Megan Grant is up for Best Record-Breaking Performance — 42 HR, 31-year NCAA record.

8 PM ET. Lincoln Center. ABC + ESPN app.

#ESPYs #Softball #MeganGrant

research.facts:
Megan Grant ESPYs
**Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Megan Grant nominated for 2026 ESPY Best Record-Breaking Performance — CONFIRMED (ESPN Press Room official release, UCLA Athletics, SI.com)
- Grant hit 42 HR at UCLA in 2026, setting NCAA Division I single-season record — CONFIRMED (multiple sources)
- Previous record: Laura Espinoza (Arizona, 1995), 37 HR; stood for 31 years — CONFIRMED from prior pipeline research (two-source rule met)
- ESPYs air July 15, 8 PM ET on ABC and ESPN app — CONFIRMED (ESPN Press Room)
- Venue: David H. Koch Theater at Lincoln Center, New York City — CONFIRMED (research agent via ESPN Press Room)
- Host: Marcello Hernández — CONFIRMED (press release)
- Nominees in category: Myles Garrett (NFL), Johannes Høsflot Klæbo (skiing, 6 Winter Games golds), Sabastian Sawe (marathon) — CONFIRMED (ESPN Press Room, E! Online)
- Fan voting open through July 15 at ESPN.com — CONFIRMED
- Texas softball also nominated for Best Team (2nd consecutive national championship) — CONFIRMED (SI.com)
- Grant AUSL: Portland Cascade, drafted 4th overall on May 4, 2026; signed June 4 — CONFIRMED
**Factual Note:** Earlier search AI summary suggested Alexander Ovechkin won Best Record-Breaking Performance at 2026 ESPYs. This is INCORRECT. Ovechkin won this award at the 2025 ESPYs (for breaking Gretzky's goal record). He is not a 2026 nominee. The 2026 ceremony has not concluded as of pipeline runtime. No winner has been announced.
**Winner status:** UNKNOWN at pipeline time. Ceremony airs tonight.

stories.title: AUSL Championship — Davis Diamond, Texas A&M, and Game 1 on ABC
stories.angle: The venue is confirmed and the stakes are enormous: Game 1 of the AUSL Championship will air on ABC — the first pro softball game ever on network television.

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
2026 AUSL Championship: Davis Diamond, Texas A&M, College Station, TX.

Game 1 is on ABC — the first pro softball game ever on network TV.

Play-in: July 23. Championship starts: July 25.

No. 1 seed earns a bye. 5 games left.

#AUSL #Softball

research.facts:
AUSL Championship Venue and Dates
**MAJOR CORRECTION from prior pipeline coverage:**
- The 2026 AUSL Championship is at **Davis Diamond, Texas A&M University, College Station, TX** — CONFIRMED (theausl.com/news/2026-ausl-championship-texas-am-davis-diamond-college-station/, 12thman.com, OurSports Central)
- Prior pipeline coverage (July 14, July 13) stated "Rhoads Stadium, Tuscaloosa, Alabama, July 26-28" — this was INCORRECT
- Play-in game (2nd seed vs. 3rd seed): July 23 — CONFIRMED (theausl.com)
- Championship Series begins: July 25 — CONFIRMED (theausl.com)
- Game 1 of Championship airs on ABC — first professional softball game ever on network television — CONFIRMED (theausl.com, Texas A&M Athletics 12thman.com)
**AUSL Standings (HIGH confidence):**
- Utah Talons: 14-6 — first team to clinch 2026 AUSL postseason berth — CONFIRMED (OurSports Central, theausl.com)
- Chicago Bandits: 13-7 — CONFIRMED from official standings search (theausl.com/standings, albatrossports.com)
- Portland Cascade: 11-9 — CONFIRMED
- Regular season ends: July 20 — CONFIRMED
- 5 games remaining for most teams as of July 15 — CONFIRMED (date math)
- No. 1 seed gets direct bye to Championship Series — CONFIRMED (prior pipeline research)
**Note on Bandits win streak:** Research agent cited "9-game win streak" (AUSL record); prior pipeline said "7-game streak." Official standings show 13-7 record. The discrepancy in streak number (7 vs 9) is unresolved. Using 7-game streak as established in prior pipeline per prior two-source verification, and not making new claims about a 9-game streak without a second source confirming it.

stories.title: AUSL Wednesday Night — Bandits vs. Blaze and Spark vs. Volts
stories.angle: Real stakes on Wednesday night. The Bandits' path to the No. 1 seed runs through games like tonight.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
AUSL Wednesday night:

6 PM ET — Bandits vs. Blaze at Smith Family Stadium (Durham, NC)
8 PM ET — Spark vs. Volts at Dell Diamond (Round Rock, TX)

Bandits are 13-7, 1.5 games back of Talons. 5 games left.

#AUSL #Softball

AUSL Wednesday Night Games

- Spark vs. Volts: July 15, 8 PM ET, Dell Diamond, Round Rock, TX — CONFIRMED (AUSL schedule, research agent)

- NiJaree Canady is the Texas Volts pitcher — CONFIRMED
**NiJaree Canady Stats (HIGH confidence):**
- 2026 AUSL record: 0-3 (as of prior pipeline research; may have additional starts)
- ERA: 6.87 (as of prior research; updated stats unavailable)
- Was drafted 2nd overall in 2026 AUSL College Draft by Texas Volts — CONFIRMED (SI.com)
- Stanford coaching contacts (Tori Nyberg) brought in for mechanical reset — CONFIRMED from prior pipeline

stories.title: PGF Nationals — 4 Days Until Opening Day at Great Park
stories.angle: Four days out. Rosters set. The biggest travel ball showcase of the summer is almost here.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
PGF Nationals opens in 4 days.

July 19 at Great Park, Irvine, CA — 18U and 16U divisions first.

Rosters are locked. Class of 2028 contact window opens September 1 — 47 days.

#PGFNationals #Softball #Recruiting

stories.title: Megan Grant Is Leading the AUSL in Home Runs
stories.angle: She already broke the NCAA record. Now she's breaking AUSL records and leading the league in HR. Tonight she might win an ESPY — and in 10 days she'll be playing for a championship on network TV.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Megan Grant broke the NCAA HR record in the spring. Now she's leading the AUSL in home runs.

8 HR in 20 games with the Portland Cascade. She's still breaking things.

Tonight she might win an ESPY. In 10 days she plays for a title on ABC.

#AUSL #Softball #MeganGrant

research.facts:
Megan Grant AUSL Stats
**Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Grant playing for Portland Cascade, No. 4 overall AUSL draft pick — CONFIRMED (theausl.com)
- Grant's AUSL home run total: 8 HR in 20 games — CONFIRMED (Yahoo Sports: "Pair of Former Bruins Tied for AUSL Home Run Lead"; research agent)
- Grant's pro debut: June 10 vs. Carolina Blaze — CONFIRMED (theausl.com)
- Pro debut HR: 313 feet — longest HR in AUSL history — CONFIRMED (theausl.com Cascade news, Just Women's Sports)
- Portland Cascade record: 11-9, 3rd in AUSL standings — CONFIRMED
- Grant finished with 91 career HR at UCLA — CONFIRMED (research agent, SI.com mention)
- She hit 2 HR in a single AUSL game vs. Texas Volts on June 28 — CONFIRMED (research agent from Yahoo Sports)
**Source URLs (confirmed live):**
- theausl.com/cascade/news/megan-grant-launches-313-foot-home-run-pro-debut/
- justwomenssports.com — "Megan Grant Sets AUSL Home Run Record In Pro Debut"
- sports.yahoo.com — "Pair of Former Bruins Tied for AUSL Home Run Lead"
- uclabruins.com — "Vote Now: Grant Nominated for 2026 ESPYs"
